topic: A person finds out their surprise party has been cancelled
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in my inbox with a dull thud. My thumb hovered over the 'delete' button before finally clicking on 'read.' The words swam before my eyes: "Due to unforeseen circumstances, the surprise celebration for [Name] has been postponed." A heavy, cold weight settled in my chest, making it difficult to draw a full breath. I stared at the screen, the cheerful decorations I had mentally pictured crumbling into dust. A tightness gripped my throat, making swallowing feel like a deliberate, painful effort. I walked to the window, the bright afternoon light suddenly feeling harsh and unforgiving. Outside, the world continued its vibrant rhythm, utterly disconnected from the quiet ache blooming inside me.

[story 2]

I reread the message three times, the same lines echoing hollowly in the silence of my apartment. My hands began to tremble slightly, the paper crinkling under my anxious grip. It wasn't just the event; it was the anticipation, the carefully constructed excitement I had nurtured for weeks, evaporating instantly. A strange numbness crept over my limbs, a leaden feeling that made movement sluggish. I slumped onto the sofa, the cushions offering no real comfort. My gaze drifted to the framed photograph on the mantelpiece—a picture of everyone smiling—and a single, hot prickle traced a path down my cheek. I wanted to call someone, to voice the sheer deflation of the moment, but my voice caught in my throat, producing only a thin, shaky exhale.

[story 3]

When I saw the notification, the color seemed to drain from the room. It was a sharp, sudden drop, like plunging into icy water. I stood by the kitchen counter, the ceramic mug suddenly feeling impossibly heavy in my hands. My jaw tightened until my teeth ached. I didn't move to check my phone again; the information was etched into my awareness, a sharp, unwelcome truth. A slow, deep sigh escaped me, a sound that seemed too large for my frame. I turned away from the window, focusing instead on the pattern of the floor tiles, tracing them with my eyes as if searching for some hidden assurance that this reality hadn't actually happened.
